reforms.description: The Ingenio2010 plan aims to enhance Spain's research and innovation capacity by doubling the civilian research budget and introducing various instruments to stimulate technology transfers and private-sector research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding and focus on R&D are expected to enhance productivity and innovation in the private sector, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan is described as well thought out and generously funded, indicating it is in the planning stages.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Ingenio2010 plan represents a comprehensive strategy to significantly enhance Spain's R&D capabilities and innovation ecosystem, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Ingenio2010 plan, which is the first part of this strategy, is particularly detailed and generously funded, with the civilian research budget set to double during the present parliamentary term.

reforms.description: Proposed reforms to the university system aim to enhance performance appraisal linked to funding mechanisms, increase university independence, and improve the research assessment system to foster applied research and technology transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the quality of higher education and fostering applied research, these reforms are expected to enhance the skills of the workforce and promote innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: These proposals, which are awaiting examination by Parliament, are laudable.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed reforms represent a significant shift in how universities are funded and evaluated, aiming to improve their contribution to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Measures reforming university education were also recently launched to boost Spain’s relatively poor performance as exemplified by international rankings.

reforms.description: The National Reform Programme aims to increase public R&D spending by 0.4 percentage points of GDP by 2010, as part of a broader strategy to transform the economy towards productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have embarked on an ambitious programme to transform the economy and guide it towards a growth model based on productivity increases.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing public R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation and improving economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The National Reform Programme calls for increasing public R&D spending by 0.4 percentage point of GDP by 2010.

reforms.description: The Spanish authorities have approved a broad programme called Ingenio2010 aimed at boosting the country's innovation performance, addressing the significant gap in R&D expenditure and innovation activities compared to other OECD countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme is designed to enhance R&D investment and innovation capacity, which are critical for improving productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have approved a broad programme to boost innovation performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: Ingenio2010 represents a structural change in Spain's approach to innovation, aiming to significantly increase R&D investment and improve the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: In response, the authorities have approved a broad programme, Ingenio2010, in order to boost Spain’s innovation performance.

reforms.description: The Spanish government has decided on a substantial rise in the research and innovation budget, aiming to increase R&D expenditure to 2% of GDP by 2010 and raise the private sector's contribution to this expenditure by 7 percentage points.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set ambitious targets for R&D expenditure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Spain's approach to R&D funding, aiming to align with EU averages and stimulate innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has decided on a substantial rise in the research and innovation budget and has set ambitious targets.

reforms.description: The National Reform Programme includes a commitment to strengthen performance in research, development, and innovation, with measures to improve the framework conditions for R&D and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the framework for R&D and innovation is expected to facilitate better outcomes in research and technological advancements, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Programme rests on seven main pillars, including strengthening performance in research, development, and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a comprehensive approach to enhancing Spain's innovation landscape, indicating a significant commitment to improving R&D performance.
reforms.source_quote: The Programme rests on seven main pillars, including strengthening performance in research, development, and innovation.

reforms.description: A new programme called NEOTEC has been launched to support Spanish technology-based start-ups, aiming to enhance the entrepreneurial ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By supporting technology-based start-ups, the initiative is expected to foster innovation and create jobs, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The NEOTEC programme has been launched to support start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NEOTEC programme represents a significant effort to enhance the start-up ecosystem in Spain, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: A programme (NEOTEC) has been launched to support Spanish technology-based start-ups.

reforms.description: Legislation has been adopted to support innovative clusters, aiming to enhance collaboration and innovation among firms and research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration within clusters, the legislation is expected to enhance innovation and competitiveness in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: New legislation has been adopted to support innovative clusters.
reforms.importance_rationale: The legislation represents a significant step towards enhancing innovation through collaborative efforts in clusters, which is vital for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: New legislation has also been adopted in 2006 to support innovative clusters.

reforms.description: The Ingenio 2010 Plan was launched to enhance research and development, particularly in the private sector, and to promote the expansion of the information society.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D is expected to stimulate private sector innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ingenio 2010 Plan was launched to enhance R&D efforts.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Ingenio 2010 Plan represents a significant structural change in Spain's approach to R&D funding and innovation policy, aiming to align with OECD standards.
reforms.source_quote: The Ingenio 2010 Plan was launched to enhance research and development, particularly in the private sector.

reforms.description: The government has committed to doubling civilian research funding over four years as part of the Ingenio 2010 Plan, with significant budget increases planned for R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Doubling research funding is expected to enhance the capacity for innovation and improve overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to doubling civilian research funding over four years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding increase is a major reform aimed at significantly enhancing Spain's research capabilities and innovation output.
reforms.source_quote: The government has committed to doubling civilian research funding over four years as part of the Ingenio 2010 Plan.

reforms.description: The Ingenio2010 programme was established with a €1 billion budget to foster long-term cooperation between private firms and public research bodies, focusing on strategic projects that involve multiple stakeholders.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages collaboration between public and private sectors, enhancing innovation capacity and addressing market needs.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has prompted a large number of companies to ask to participate.
reforms.importance_rationale: This programme represents a significant structural change in how public and private sectors collaborate on R&D, aiming for ambitious outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: One of the main objectives of the Ingenio2010 programme is to encourage collaboration between private and public research groups.

reforms.description: The Spanish government has shifted its focus from subsidised loans to direct subsidies for private research and innovation, alongside a proposed reduction in social insurance contributions for research workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Direct subsidies are expected to enhance the financial support for private R&D, encouraging more firms to invest in innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The focus of the instruments designed to stimulate private research and innovation is being shifted to subsidies rather than subsidised loans.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in funding strategy, aiming to improve the effectiveness of financial support for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The focus of the instruments designed to stimulate private research and innovation is being shifted to subsidies rather than subsidised loans.

reforms.description: A new programme (Avanz@) was launched to promote the diffusion of ICTs in businesses and households, providing substantial funding for technology investments and training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing ICT access and training, the programme aims to improve productivity and innovation capabilities in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan provides for subsidised credits to be made available to private individuals to encourage Internet access.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in ICT infrastructure and training, which is crucial for modernizing the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Avanz@ plan, which sets itself ambitious targets in terms of ICT distribution, also contains non-budgetary measures.

reforms.description: The Spanish government is modifying the framework law on universities to grant greater autonomy to universities, allowing them to define teaching programs and recruit professors, while also enhancing the monitoring and assessment of quality through transparent criteria.
reforms.package_name: University Autonomy and Quality Improvement Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing university autonomy and quality assessment, the reform aims to improve educational outcomes, thereby increasing the supply of skilled labor and fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The modification of the framework law is currently being discussed in Parliament.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the governance of higher education, aiming to enhance quality and responsiveness to labor market needs, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A modification of the framework law on universities, which is currently being discussed in Parliament, deals with three main issues.

reforms.description: The government is encouraging the establishment of joint research institutes between universities and businesses to enhance collaboration and facilitate personnel exchanges, as well as allowing professors to take voluntary leave to engage in applied projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between academia and industry, the reform aims to enhance the commercialization of research and improve the relevance of academic work to market needs.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform encourages the creation of joint research institutes with businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift towards integrating academic research with industry needs, which is essential for driving innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Universities and public research centres will also be able to create joint research institutes with businesses in order to forge closer links and facilitate personnel exchanges.

reforms.description: The government has launched a support plan for entrepreneurship (Plan de Fomento Empresal, PFE) aimed at encouraging firm creation and growth, particularly for young and technology-based companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to enhance the entrepreneurial environment, facilitating access to credit and resources for startups, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The PFE was initiated to address the challenges faced by entrepreneurs and SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in supporting entrepreneurship, which is crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The PFE encourages the spirit of enterprise by means of measures aimed in a number of directions.

reforms.description: The government has increased the budget for subsidies to SMEs, enhancing their access to credit and supporting their modernization efforts.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing financial support for SMEs, the reform aims to stimulate innovation and competitiveness within the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The total amount of aid for SMEs was budgeted at €8 billion in 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: This increase in subsidies is a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms for SMEs, aimed at fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The total amount of such aid... was budgeted at €8 billion in 2006.

reforms.description: The NEOTEC venture capital program was launched with a funding of €176 million to promote the creation of technology-based start-ups in Spain through investments in venture-capital companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the creation of technology-based start-ups, the program aims to enhance innovation and economic growth through increased entrepreneurial activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The program was launched with specific funding to support start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NEOTEC program represents a significant investment in fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: A government programme (NEOTEC venture capital) with funding of €176 million has been launched to cover the period 2006-2013 and promote the creation of Spanish technology-based start-ups.

reforms.description: The reform of innovation policy based on the Ingenio2010 Plan aims to improve Spain's innovation performance through a comprehensive strategy involving various instruments and substantial funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing multiple fronts of innovation policy, the plan is expected to enhance productivity and innovation outcomes in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform strategy is designed to increase the effectiveness of innovation policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Ingenio2010 Plan represents a comprehensive and ambitious approach to reforming innovation policy, which is critical for addressing Spain's innovation challenges.
reforms.source_quote: Improving the Spanish economy’s innovation performance... rests on a comprehensive reform strategy, since it is designed to increase the effectiveness of innovation policy proper.

reforms.description: The government is considering abolishing restrictions that limit principal researchers to one government-backed project, which could enhance collaboration and innovation in research teams.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Removing project restrictions is expected to foster collaboration and increase the number of innovative projects undertaken by researchers.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering this reform as part of its innovation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant barrier to research collaboration, potentially leading to increased innovation output.
reforms.source_quote: Consider reducing the splitting up of research teams by abolishing the restrictions whereby there can be only one government-backed project per principal researcher.

reforms.description: The governance of public research centres is proposed to be improved to ensure they have sufficient budgetary and administrative independence, which is crucial for effective research management.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing governance will allow research centres to operate more efficiently, leading to better research outcomes and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Recommendations aimed at improving governance are presented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses structural issues in public research management that can enhance overall research effectiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the governance of public research centres so as to ensure that they have sufficient budgetary and administrative independence.

reforms.description: The proposal includes enhancing the ability of technology transfer offices to improve technology transfers by organizing them into networks, which could facilitate better collaboration between research and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving technology transfer capabilities will enhance the commercialization of research outputs, benefiting both the economy and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Improve technology transfers through increasing the ability of technology transfer offices to act by grouping them together or organising them in networks.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is important as it aims to strengthen the link between research and industry, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Improve technology transfers through increasing the ability of technology transfer offices to act by grouping them together or organising them in networks.

reforms.description: The government plans to improve coordination among innovation-oriented programmes to facilitate access to information for firms, particularly SMEs, which could enhance their innovation capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Better coordination will streamline access to resources and information, enabling firms to innovate more effectively.
reforms.status_evidence: Improve co-ordination among innovation-oriented programmes within central government and among the regions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the effectiveness of existing innovation programmes, which is crucial for fostering a vibrant innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Improve co-ordination among innovation-oriented programmes within central government and among the regions.

reforms.description: The IDE initiative, replaced by the Torres Quevedo programme, aims to encourage firms to employ research staff by subsidizing their recruitment for a two-year period, resulting in significant job creation in the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By subsidizing the recruitment of skilled researchers, the programme enhances firms' innovation capabilities and fosters collaboration with public research centres.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has expanded in scope and resulted in the recruitment of 1782 researchers in the private sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme significantly impacts the employment of researchers in the private sector, enhancing innovation activities, although it has some deadweight loss.
reforms.source_quote: The Torres Quevedo programme, which replaced the IDE initiative, aims to encourage firms to employ research staff.

reforms.description: Technology Centres (TCs) provide technological services to firms, particularly SMEs, fostering innovation through R&D projects and technology transfers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing firms' access to technological services, TCs improve their innovation performance and competitiveness in the global market.
reforms.status_evidence: Technology Centres play a crucial role in the improvement of innovation performance of enterprises.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative supports a wide range of firms, particularly SMEs, in enhancing their innovation capabilities, contributing to regional economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Technology Centres play a crucial role in the improvement of innovation performance of enterprises, especially SMEs.

reforms.description: Spain's authorities have adopted measures to address the shortfall in innovation and the use of new technologies, aiming to strengthen entrepreneurship and improve the education system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are expected to enhance the entrepreneurial environment, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have adopted measures to make up for Spain’s shortfall in innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards fostering innovation and entrepreneurship, which are critical for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have adopted measures to make up for Spain’s shortfall in innovation and the use of new technologies, strengthen entrepreneurship and bolster the education system.

reforms.description: The government aims to foster entrepreneurship by reducing administrative barriers to new enterprise creation and improving access to finance for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering barriers and enhancing access to finance, the initiative is expected to stimulate new business formation and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to foster entrepreneurship through various measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the entrepreneurial environment, which is crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Foster entrepreneurship by reducing administrative barriers to new enterprise creation and improving access to finance.

reforms.description: The Spanish government aims to increase public R&D spending from 0.5% to 0.9% of GDP by 2010 as part of its National Reform Programme, reflecting a commitment to enhance the country's research capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities' objective is to raise public R&D spending from 0.5% to 0.9% of GDP by 2010.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation and enhancing the overall competitiveness of the Spanish economy.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities’ objective is to raise public R&D spending from 0.5% to 0.9% of GDP by 2010.

reforms.description: The updated Fiscal Stability Law includes provisions that allow for exceptions to deficit restrictions for increases in productive investment programmes, including R&D, which are deemed to improve productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By allowing exceptions for R&D investments, the reform encourages public and private sector collaboration in enhancing productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The updated law has been enacted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in fiscal policy, allowing for targeted investment in R&D, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The new Fiscal Stability Law aims for fiscal stability over the economic cycle... a special allowance is made to exceptionally exclude increases in investment in programmes for productive activities, R&D included.

reforms.description: The Ingenio2010 Plan includes measures to enhance research excellence and increase the size of research teams by establishing public research funding based on strategic guidelines, promoting large-scale teams and consortia, and launching an Activation Plan for the 7th Framework Programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing research funding and collaboration, the plan aims to improve scientific output and innovation capacity, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been launched with specific measures and funding allocations.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Ingenio2010 Plan represents a significant structural change in public research funding aimed at enhancing Spain's research capabilities and international collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: The Ingenio2010 Plan includes three main types of measures aiming at enhancing the excellence and increasing the size of research teams.

reforms.description: A €1 billion fund has been created to ensure the availability and renewal of government scientific resources over four years, aimed at enhancing research capabilities in Spain.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public investment in scientific research, which can lead to higher innovation and productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public funding for scientific research, which is crucial for fostering innovation and enhancing Spain's research landscape.
reforms.source_quote: A €1 billion fund (0.1% of GDP) over four years has been created to ensure the availability and renewal of government scientific resources.

reforms.description: The authorities have introduced contracts to recruit confirmed researchers to stable jobs, allowing additional teachers to be hired and freeing top researchers from teaching duties.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the capacity of research institutions by allowing top researchers to focus on research, thereby improving innovation output.
reforms.status_evidence: Contracts have been introduced and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the recruitment and retention of skilled researchers, which is vital for enhancing research quality and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Contracts will be introduced that make it possible to recruit to a stable job confirmed Spanish or foreign researchers who are not civil servants.
